title: Kim Long Motor and BYD to Build $130 Million EV Battery Plant in Vietnam
content: Vietnam's Kim Long Motor is partnering with Chinese EV giant BYD to establish a $130 million electric vehicle (EV) battery plant in central Vietnam. Under the agreement, Kim Long Motor will finance the construction, while BYD will provide comprehensive technical support. The facility, initially covering 4.4 hectares, will have a capacity of 3 gigawatt-hours (GWh) per year, focusing on batteries for commercial vehicles such as electric buses, trucks, and minibuses. A planned second phase aims to expand the site to 10 hectares and double capacity to 6 GWh annually, extending production to include passenger car batteries. This collaboration comes as Vietnam's EV market accelerates, currently led by VinFast. The project underscores the growing regional push for cleaner transport infrastructure and supply chain localization, although a specific start date for operations has not yet been announced.
author: Analysts Account
